explanation.one_sentence: This repository is a compact demo workflow that turns one image into editable RGBA layers, lets users refine those layers, and then recombines or exports the results.
what_it_does: Decomposes an image into multiple transparent layers
Lets users edit individual layers with a text-guided image editor
Recombines layer stacks into a final image
Exports outputs in gallery, ZIP, PPTX, and PSD formats

how_it_is_organized.documentation: README.md explains installation and how to run each stage

explanation.how_to_think_about_it: Treat the repo as a three-stage operator workflow rather than as a library: produce layers, optionally revise them, then merge them back together.
important_details: The three scripts are related by user workflow more than by shared code.
The main app contains the most substantial logic and export functionality.
The project depends on external pretrained model assets and likely expects GPU access.
Some snapshot details are incomplete, including missing assets and no pinned dependency manifest.
likely_use_cases: Exporting generated layers into presentation or design tools
